source_pr.title: server: use TempDir for plan replayer's UT
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #62441

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Because the paths of the plan replayer are all fixed, different tests will clean up the corresponding directories after completion, causing files generated by tests that are still running to be deleted, leading to flaky tests.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Because the paths of the plan replayer are all fixed, different tests will clean up the corresponding directories after completion, causing files generated by tests that are still running to be deleted, leading to flaky tests.
failure_signature: Because the paths of the plan replayer are all fixed, different tests will clean up the corresponding directories after completion, causing files generated by tests that are still running to be deleted, leading to flaky tests.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: Because the paths of the plan replayer are all fixed, different tests will clean up the corresponding directories after completion, causing files generated by tests that are still running to be deleted, leading to flaky….
fix_pattern: Because the paths of the plan replayer are all fixed, different tests will clean up the corresponding directories after completion, causing files generated by tests that are still running to be deleted, leading to flaky tests.

evidence: evidence: Because the paths of the plan replayer are all fixed, different tests will clean up the corresponding directories after completion, causing files generated by tests that are still running to be deleted, leading to flaky tests.
